title: Street Art
description: For questions 9 – 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Street art (0) has transformed from an underground movement into a respected art form that attracts millions of admirers worldwide. What was once considered vandalism is now celebrated in galleries and museums across the globe.||The rise of street art began (9) in the 1970s in New York City, where artists used spray paint to create colourful murals on subway trains and buildings. Over time, street art (10) has evolved to include various techniques and styles, from stencils and posters to large-scale murals. Some street artists, (11) such as Banksy, have achieved international fame despite remaining anonymous.||Today, many cities actively encourage street art (12) by designating legal walls where artists can work freely. This (13) has led to the creation of vibrant neighbourhoods that attract tourists and support local businesses. Street art festivals are held regularly, bringing together artists (14) from around the world to collaborate and share their work. Critics argue that street art should remain rebellious and anti-establishment, (15) while supporters believe that recognition helps artists earn a living. Whatever your view, there's no denying (16) that street art has become an integral part of contemporary urban culture.

example.text: Street art (0) transformed from an underground movement into a respected art form.

questions.explanation: Preposición 'in' se usa con décadas: 'in the 1970s'.
Presente perfecto: 'has evolved' indica cambio hasta el presente.
Expresión 'such as' introduce ejemplos específicos de artistas.
Preposición 'by' introduce el método para fomentar el arte.
Presente perfecto: 'has led to' indica resultado de una acción.
Preposición 'from' indica el origen de los artistas.
Conjunción que contrasta dos opiniones diferentes.
Conjunción 'that' introduce una cláusula nominal después de 'denying'.
